records.ety: [Contr, from OF. manuvrer, manovrer, to work with the hand, to cultivate by manual labor, F. manœuvrer. See Manual, Ure, Opera, and cf. Inure.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Manured (m*nūrd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Manuring.]

defs: To cultivate by manual labor; to till; hence, to develop by culture.
quotes: To whom we gave the strand for to manure.
Manure thyself then; to thyself be improved; And with vain, outward things be no more moved.

defs: To apply manure to; to enrich, as land, by the application of a fertilizing substance.
quotes: The blood of English shall manure the ground.

defs: Any matter which makes land productive; a fertilizing substance.
dung, the contents of stables and barnyards, decaying animal or vegetable substances, etc.
